wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Multi-Tenant Car Auction System project overview. Explain the purpose of this luxury car auction platform, its dual-brand architecture supporting HM CAR and CAR X, and the overall system design. Document the technology stack including Node.js/Express backend, Next.js frontend applications, MongoDB database, and real-time communication via WebSockets. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating the multi-tenant architecture and shared infrastructure. Document the relationship between the two frontend applications and their distinct branding while sharing common backend services.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Introduction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content introducing the Multi-Tenant Car Auction System. Explain the project's purpose as a luxury car auction platform supporting both HM CAR and CAR X brand identities. Document the dual-brand architecture concept, explaining how two distinct frontend applications share common backend infrastructure while maintaining separate branding and user experiences. Include the system's core value proposition, target audience, and business model. Provide an overview of the platform's capabilities including real-time bidding, vehicle listings, spare parts marketplace, and administrative management. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and explain the relationship between the two frontend applications and shared backend services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Tenant Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the multi-tenant car auction system design. Describe the tenant isolation strategy, database connection management per tenant, and tenant-specific configuration handling. Document the tenant resolution mechanism that distinguishes between HM CAR and CAR X brand identities. Explain the shared vs separate resource architecture, including how common services are reused across tenants while maintaining data isolation. Detail the tenant middleware implementation, database manager patterns, and configuration inheritance. Include tenant registration workflows, dynamic tenant switching, and performance optimization strategies for multi-tenant scaling. Address tenant-specific customization capabilities, branding isolation, and administrative controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core Express.js application setup. Detail the App class architecture, middleware configuration, route registration, and error handling mechanisms. Explain the serverless deployment considerations for Vercel, CORS configuration differences between serverless and traditional deployments, and the modular middleware stack. Document the startup sequence, graceful shutdown procedures, and monitoring integration. Include practical examples of extending the application, adding custom middleware, and configuring different deployment environments. Address performance optimization techniques, security headers implementation, and request tracking mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Business Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for core business services that handle primary business logic. Document the InventoryService for managing car listings, spare parts, and stock management including CRUD operations, availability tracking, and inventory optimization. Cover the PaymentService integration with payment gateways, transaction processing, refund handling, and payment verification workflows. Explain the AnalyticsService for tracking user behavior, conversion metrics, and business insights with data collection and reporting mechanisms. Detail the SeedService for initial data population, database initialization, and system setup procedures. Include service architecture patterns, error handling strategies, and integration examples with the broader system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the car auction system. Include step-by-step installation instructions for both HM CAR and CAR X frontend applications, backend setup, database configuration, and environment variable setup. Document prerequisite software requirements, development environment configuration, and initial project setup. Provide clear instructions for running the applications locally, connecting to MongoDB instances, and accessing the admin panels. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation. Make the content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for the Multi-Tenant Car Auction System. Document the full technology stack including Node.js/Express backend, Next.js applications for both HM CAR and CAR X, MongoDB database with Mongoose ODM, and real-time communication via Socket.IO. Detail the frontend frameworks (Next.js with TypeScript), styling libraries (Tailwind CSS), and mobile development stack (React Native with Capacitor). Include external service integrations such as Cloudinary for image storage, Sentry for error tracking, and various authentication providers. Document the deployment technologies including Docker, Vercel hosting, and Nginx configuration. Explain the rationale behind technology choices and their role in supporting the multi-tenant architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-Time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the real-time communication infrastructure. Describe the WebSocket implementation using Socket.IO, event broadcasting patterns, and room management strategies. Document the real-time auction system including live bidding updates, bid notifications, and auction state synchronization. Explain the integration between WebSocket events and REST API endpoints, message queuing, and client-side reconnection strategies. Detail the event-driven architecture for live notifications, push updates, and system alerts. Include performance considerations for concurrent connections, memory management, and scaling WebSocket infrastructure. Address security aspects of real-time communication, authentication for WebSocket connections, and rate limiting for real-time events.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Tenant System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the multi-tenant architecture implementation. Explain tenant configuration management, database connection isolation, and tenant resolution logic. Document the tenant resolver algorithm, database switching mechanisms, and tenant-specific resource management. Include practical examples of adding new tenants, configuring tenant-specific settings, and implementing tenant-aware business logic. Address database indexing strategies for multi-tenant queries, performance considerations for tenant isolation, and security measures to prevent tenant data leakage. Provide troubleshooting guides for common multi-tenant issues and best practices for scaling across multiple tenants.

wiki_catalogs.name: Communication Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for communication services that handle user notifications and external messaging. Document the EmailService integration with SMTP providers, email template management, bulk email processing, and delivery tracking. Cover the WhatsAppService for automated messaging, customer communication workflows, and message scheduling. Explain the NotificationService for push notifications, in-app alerts, and real-time messaging with WebSocket integration. Detail the SmartAlertService for intelligent alerting, threshold monitoring, and automated user notifications. Include configuration examples, error handling, retry mechanisms, and best practices for reliable communication delivery.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the multi-tenant car auction system. Describe the high-level design patterns including microservices architecture, real-time communication infrastructure, and tenant isolation mechanisms. Document component interactions between frontend applications, backend services, database layer, and external integrations. Explain technical decisions around multi-tenant database isolation, shared vs separate resources, and performance optimization strategies. Include infrastructure requirements, scalability considerations, and deployment topology. Provide system context diagrams showing the relationship between HM CAR and CAR X applications sharing common backend services. Address cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and tenant-specific customization capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Tenant Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the multi-tenant architecture implementation. Explain how the system supports multiple brand identities (HM CAR and CAR X) while sharing common backend infrastructure. Document the tenant resolution mechanism, database connection management per tenant, and isolation strategies. Detail the tenant configuration system, including how brand-specific settings, themes, and features are managed. Explain the database schema design that supports tenant isolation while enabling shared functionality. Include practical examples of tenant-specific customizations, feature flags, and configuration overrides. Document the technical implementation of multi-tenancy including connection pooling, query scoping, and data segregation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Microservices Pattern
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the microservices architecture pattern implementation. Describe the modular service separation including authentication service, inventory management service, notification service, and caching service. Document the service boundary definitions, inter-service communication patterns, and dependency management. Explain the service discovery mechanism, load balancing strategies, and fault tolerance approaches. Detail the configuration management across services, shared utilities, and common infrastructure components. Include service orchestration, monitoring integration, and distributed tracing capabilities. Address service versioning, backward compatibility, and deployment strategies for independent service scaling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation covering JWT token management, user registration/login workflows, permission systems, and session handling. Detail the authentication middleware implementation, role-based access control (RBAC), and permission validation mechanisms. Document user registration processes, password hashing, email verification, and two-factor authentication integration. Include practical examples of implementing custom permissions, extending authentication flows, and securing API endpoints. Address security best practices, token refresh mechanisms, session management, and protection against common authentication attacks. Provide troubleshooting guides for authentication failures and permission-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for infrastructure services that support system performance and external integrations. Document the CacheService implementation with Redis integration, cache strategies, invalidation policies, and performance optimization techniques. Cover the CDNService for content delivery optimization, asset caching, and global distribution strategies. Explain the externalImageService for cloud storage integration, image optimization, and media processing workflows. Detail the imageProcessingService for automatic resizing, format conversion, and quality optimization. Document the WebSocketService for real-time communication, connection management, and event broadcasting. Include configuration examples, monitoring approaches, and troubleshooting guides for each service.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend development documentation for the car auction system. Cover the Express.js application setup, middleware stack configuration, API endpoint organization, and real-time communication implementation. Document the multi-tenant middleware system, authentication and authorization mechanisms, and database interaction patterns. Include detailed coverage of the service layer architecture, business logic implementation, and external service integrations. Provide practical examples of extending the API, adding new endpoints, implementing custom middleware, and integrating third-party services. Address performance optimization, error handling strategies, and security best practices for backend development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment strategy documentation for the Multi-Tenant Car Auction System. Document the deployment architecture supporting both HM CAR and CAR X on the same infrastructure with different domains and branding. Detail the Vercel deployment configuration, Docker containerization approach, and PM2 process management. Explain the environment variable management for multiple tenants, including database connections and API keys. Document the CI/CD pipeline, automated deployment scripts, and rollback procedures. Include infrastructure requirements, scaling considerations, and monitoring setup. Provide step-by-step deployment guides for both development and production environments, including domain configuration and SSL certificate management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the data flow architecture throughout the system. Describe the request processing pipeline from incoming HTTP requests through middleware layers to database operations. Document the data transformation flows, caching strategies, and response formatting patterns. Explain the tenant-aware data routing, validation pipelines, and error propagation mechanisms. Detail the integration points between frontend applications, API endpoints, and database models. Include data consistency strategies, transaction management, and eventual consistency patterns. Address performance optimization through caching layers, database indexing strategies, and query optimization techniques. Document audit trails, data logging, and monitoring integration points.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-Time Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the real-time communication system using Socket.IO. Explain WebSocket connection management, room-based communication, event broadcasting, and client-side integration patterns. Document the socket initialization process, connection handling, message routing, and real-time event management. Include practical examples of implementing custom real-time features, managing user connections, and handling disconnections gracefully. Address performance optimization for real-time features, scaling WebSocket connections, and monitoring real-time traffic. Provide troubleshooting guides for connection issues, event delivery problems, and WebSocket performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Services
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for utility services that provide system support and operational capabilities. Document the LoggerService for structured logging, log aggregation, and debugging workflows with different log levels and output formats. Cover the MonitoringService for system health checks, performance metrics, and alerting mechanisms. Include configuration examples, integration patterns, and best practices for maintaining system reliability and security.

Document the component architecture, state management patterns, styling strategies using Tailwind CSS, and internationalization support. Explain the shared component library, brand-specific customizations, and responsive design implementation. Document the build process, deployment configuration, and performance optimization techniques. Address cross-browser compatibility, accessibility compliance, and mobile-first design principles used throughout the applications.

Detail the infrastructure scaling patterns, auto-scaling configurations, and resource allocation strategies. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation covering all RESTful endpoints organized by feature groups. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for caching strategies and implementation patterns used throughout the service layer. Provide troubleshooting guides for connection issues, query performance problems, and data consistency challenges. Document backup procedures, database monitoring, and disaster recovery planning.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and support documentation for the car auction system. Include escalation procedures and support contact information for production issues.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# HM CAR - Premium Car Auction Platform

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
HM CAR is a comprehensive premium car auction platform designed to facilitate luxury vehicle sales, spare parts trading, and real-time bidding systems. The platform operates as a multi-tenant system supporting multiple brand identities while maintaining centralized management capabilities.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Multi-Tenant Architecture**: Support multiple brand identities (HM CAR and CAR X) with isolated data and custom theming
- **Real-Time Auctions**: Provide live bidding functionality with WebSocket integration
- **Premium Experience**: Deliver luxury car showcase with advanced filtering, comparison, and viewing capabilities
- **Global Operations**: Support international currency conversion and multi-language interface
- **Mobile-First Approach**: Offer native mobile applications alongside web interfaces

### Target Audience
- Luxury car buyers and sellers
- Automotive dealerships and importers
- International customers seeking premium vehicles
- Mobile-first users requiring offline capabilities

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Applications] --> B[API Gateway]
    B --> C[Express Server]
    C --> D[MongoDB Database]
    C --> E[Redis Cache]
    C --> F[Cloudinary Storage]
    
    G[Next.js Frontend] --> B
    H[Mobile App] --> B
    I[Admin Panel] --> B
    
    J[WebSocket Server] --> C
    K[Payment Services] --> C
    L[Email Service] --> C
    M[Notification Service] --> C
```

### Design Patterns
- **Multi-Tenant Pattern**: Isolated database connections per tenant with shared infrastructure
- **Microservices Architecture**: Modular services for authentication, inventory, auctions, and notifications
- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Real-time updates via WebSocket connections
- **Repository Pattern**: Data access abstraction through Mongoose models
- **Middleware Pattern**: Centralized request processing and validation

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    Client[Client Application] --> API[REST API]
    API --> Auth[Authentication Service]
    API --> Inventory[Inventory Management]
    API --> Auctions[Auction System]
    API --> Payments[Payment Processing]
    
    Auth --> Users[User Models]
    Inventory --> Cars[Car Models]
    Auctions --> Bids[Bid Models]
    Payments --> Orders[Order Models]
    
    API --> Cache[Redis Cache]
    API --> Logs[Winston Logger]
    API --> Monitor[Sentry Monitoring]
```

### Data Flow Diagrams

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant API
    participant Auth
    participant DB
    
    Client->>+API: Login Request
    API->>+Auth: Validate Credentials
    Auth->>+DB: Check User Existence
    DB-->>-Auth: User Data
    Auth-->>-API: JWT Token
    API-->>-Client: Authentication Success
    
    Client->>+API: Car Listing Request
    API->>+DB: Query Cars
    DB-->>-API: Car Data
    API-->>-Client: Car Listings
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Server Entry**: `server.js` - Main application entry point with graceful startup
- **Express App**: `modules/app.js` - Central Express application configuration
- **API Routes**: `routes/api/v2/index.js` - Version 2 API endpoint routing
- **Frontend Apps**: 
  - `carx-system/src/app/layout.tsx` - CAR X Next.js frontend
  - `client-app/src/app/layout.tsx` - HM CAR Next.js frontend

### Core Modules
- **Multi-Tenant System**: `tenants/` - Tenant resolution and database management
- **Middleware Stack**: `middleware/` - Security, rate limiting, and tenant handling
- **Service Layer**: `services/` - Business logic and external integrations
- **Data Models**: `models/` - Mongoose schemas for all entities

### Configuration Approach
- **Centralized Config**: `config/serverConfig.js` - Unified server configuration
- **Environment Variables**: `.env` files for different deployment environments
- **Docker Support**: `Dockerfile` - Containerized deployment configuration
- **Deployment Scripts**: `deploy.sh` and `ecosystem.config.js` - Automated deployment

### External Dependencies
- **Database**: MongoDB with Mongoose ODM
- **Real-time**: Socket.IO for live auction updates
- **Authentication**: JWT tokens with Firebase Admin integration
- **Storage**: Cloudinary for image management
- **Monitoring**: Sentry for error tracking and Winston for logging

### Integration Points
- **Payment Processing**: Stripe integration for secure transactions
- **WhatsApp Integration**: Direct customer communication
- **Google Analytics**: Performance and user behavior tracking
- **Push Notifications**: Web push and mobile notification services

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App Controller] --> B[Middleware Layer]
    B --> C[Route Handlers]
    C --> D[Service Layer]
    D --> E[Data Access Layer]
    E --> F[Database]
    
    G[Context Providers] --> H[UI Components]
    H --> I[State Management]
    I --> J[API Communication]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Multi-Tenant Support**: Separate branding, theming, and data isolation
- **Real-Time Bidding**: Live auction participation with instant bid updates
- **Advanced Search**: Multi-criteria filtering with text search capabilities
- **Mobile Applications**: Native apps for iOS and Android with offline capabilities
- **Admin Dashboard**: Comprehensive management interface for all operations
- **Multi-Currency**: Real-time exchange rate integration
- **Multi-Language**: Arabic and English support with RTL layout

### Implementation Highlights
- **Tenant Resolution**: `middleware/tenantMiddleware.js` - Dynamic tenant identification
- **Smart Caching**: `services/cache/` - Intelligent caching strategies for performance
- **Security Layer**: `middleware/securityEnhanced.js` - Advanced protection mechanisms
- **WebSocket Integration**: `modules/socket.js` - Real-time communication infrastructure
- **Image Processing**: `services/imageProcessingService.js` - Optimized image handling

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticating: Login Attempt
    Authenticating --> Authenticated: Success
    Authenticating --> Unauthenticated: Failed
    
    Authenticated --> Browsing: Browse Vehicles
    Authenticated --> PlacingBid: Place Bid
    Authenticated --> ManagingProfile: Manage Account
    
    Browsing --> ViewingDetails: Select Vehicle
    ViewingDetails --> PlacingBid: Place Bid
    ViewingDetails --> Browsing: Continue Browsing
    
    PlacingBid --> BidPlaced: Success
    PlacingBid --> Browsing: Cancel
    
    BidPlaced --> [*]: Complete
```

### Performance Optimization Features
- **Auto-Cache Middleware**: Intelligent caching for frequently accessed data
- **Database Indexing**: Optimized queries with strategic indexing
- **Image Optimization**: Cloudinary integration for responsive image delivery
- **Lazy Loading**: Progressive loading of components and assets
- **Service Worker**: Offline capability and background synchronization

### Security Implementation
- **Multi-Factor Authentication**: Enhanced security for admin accounts
- **Device Binding**: Prevent unauthorized device usage
- **Rate Limiting**: Protection against abuse and DDoS attacks
- **CORS Configuration**: Secure cross-origin resource sharing
- **Input Validation**: Comprehensive data sanitization and validation

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [server.js](server.js)
- [modules/app.js](modules/app.js)
- [routes/api/v2/index.js](routes/api/v2/index.js)
- [models/User.js](models/User.js)
- [models/Car.js](models/Car.js)
- [tenants/tenants.json](tenants/tenants.json)
- [config/serverConfig.js](config/serverConfig.js)
- [middleware/tenantMiddleware.js](middleware/tenantMiddleware.js)
- [services/SeedService.js](services/SeedService.js)
- [carx-system/src/app/layout.tsx](carx-system/src/app/layout.tsx)
- [client-app/src/app/layout.tsx](client-app/src/app/layout.tsx)
- [Dockerfile](Dockerfile)

</blog>
